title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, Preconditions, and Status · Contract Lens: Resource Representation Storage
summary: A task API uses POST for every action, changes state on GET, returns 200 for rejected work, and retries duplicate writes without resource or precondition semantics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts resource representation storage increment in resource, method, status, validator, and retry contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate resource identity, server state, selected representation, transfer content, TypeScript model, and PostgreSQL rows.
Handle termination signals with idempotent draining, bounded cleanup, rejection tracking, exit-code ownership, and forced-stop evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6431 in a canary revision draining long-lived response streams. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, preconditions, status, and durable effect; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
